articles.adaptedFrom: Asia Research News / Lingnan University Hong Kong
articles.title: Hong Kong Scientists Discover How Wrinkles Could Power Future Technology
articles.summary: Researchers at Lingnan University in Hong Kong have been exploring a surprising new use for wrinkled materials. Usually, wrinkles on a surface are seen as a sign of damage, but scientists have found that the patterns created by wrinkles and dents can actually be very useful. Their research shows these textured surfaces could one day be used in anti-counterfeiting labels to stop fake goods, in artificial organs that need to stretch and flex, and even in bendable batteries that can be folded like paper. This exciting discovery opens new doors for future technology and medicine across Hong Kong and the world.

lv1: What did scientists at Lingnan University discover about wrinkled materials? (嶺南大學的科學家發現皺摺材料有什麼用途？)
Name one thing that wrinkled materials could be used for in the future. (說出皺摺材料未來可以用於哪一個範疇？)
lv2: Why do you think scientists usually consider wrinkles on materials to be a problem, and what made them change their minds? (為什麼科學家通常認為材料上的皺摺是問題？是什麼令他們改變了想法？)
How could bendable batteries made from wrinkled materials change the way we use technology every day? (由皺摺材料製成的可彎曲電池，會如何改變我們日常使用科技的方式？)
lv3: Should governments invest more public money in basic scientific research like this, even when results may not be useful for many years? Argue both sides. (政府是否應該投放更多公共資源在這類基礎科學研究上，即使成果可能多年後才能應用？試從正反兩方面討論。)

Anti-counterfeiting technology helps stop fake goods, but some argue that stricter laws are more effective than technology solutions. Which approach do you think is better, and why? (防偽科技有助打擊假貨，但有人認為嚴格法律比科技方案更有效。你認為哪種方法較好？為什麼？)

articles.title: SpaceX Moves Closer to Going Public in One of the Biggest Tech Listings Ever
articles.summary: SpaceX, the private rocket company founded by Elon Musk, is moving forward with plans for an Initial Public Offering, or IPO, which would allow ordinary people to buy shares in the company. This is big news in the world of technology and business, as SpaceX is best known for building reusable rockets and sending astronauts to the International Space Station. An IPO would make SpaceX one of the largest technology companies ever to go public. Investors around the world are watching closely because the company has ambitious plans to send humans to Mars and to expand its Starlink internet service across Asia and beyond.

lv1: What is SpaceX mainly known for making and doing? (SpaceX主要以製造和從事什麼而聞名？)
What does IPO stand for, and what does it allow people to do? (IPO是什麼的縮寫？它讓人們可以做什麼？)
lv2: Why might ordinary people be interested in buying shares in a company like SpaceX? (為什麼普通市民可能有興趣購買SpaceX這類公司的股份？)
How could SpaceX's Starlink internet service benefit people living in remote parts of Asia? (SpaceX的星鏈互聯網服務如何幫助居住在亞洲偏遠地區的人？)
lv3: Some people believe that space exploration should be led by governments rather than private companies. Do you agree? What are the advantages and disadvantages of private companies like SpaceX being in charge of space travel? (有人認為太空探索應由政府主導，而非私人公司。你是否同意？私人公司主導太空旅行有何優缺點？)

If SpaceX successfully sends humans to Mars, who should have the right to govern and own resources on Mars — the company, one country, or all of humanity? (若SpaceX成功將人類送上火星，誰應擁有管治火星及其資源的權利——該公司、某一國家，還是全人類？)

articles.title: 夏寶龍視察北部都會區　促加快創科發展建設
articles.summary: 國務院港澳辦主任夏寶龍近日到香港北部都會區考察，強調要加快推進創新科技相關的建設工作。特區政府官員包括創新科技及工業局局長孫東和行政長官陳國基均表示，中央政府高度重視北部都會區，視之為香港未來發展的重要引擎。北都的發展計劃包括建立創科園區，吸引科技企業進駐，為香港年輕人提供更多與科技相關的就業機會，有助推動香港邁向「新型工業化」的目標。

lv1: 夏寶龍視察香港哪個地區？
北部都會區的發展對香港年輕人有什麼好處？
lv2: 為什麼中央政府和特區政府都十分重視北部都會區的創科發展？
「新型工業化」是什麼意思？你認為這對香港的未來有何影響？
lv3: 有人認為香港應專注發展金融業，而非花大量資源在創科上；亦有人認為創科才是香港未來的出路。你支持哪個方向？請說明理由。
若大量科技企業進駐北部都會區，可能對當地居民的生活環境帶來哪些正面和負面的影響？

articles.title: 巴基斯坦宣布伊朗同意重開霍爾木茲海峽　國際局勢出現轉機
articles.summary: 巴基斯坦總理謝里夫於6月18日宣布，伊朗已同意重新開放霍爾木茲海峽，美國亦將同步解除對該地區的封鎖行動。霍爾木茲海峽是全球最重要的石油運輸航道之一，關閉期間令國際油價大幅上升。這項協議有望緩和持續三個月的美伊緊張局勢。國際社會正密切關注協議能否順利落實，以及對全球能源供應和各地物價的長遠影響。

lv1: 是哪個國家宣布伊朗同意重開霍爾木茲海峽？
霍爾木茲海峽對全球有什麼重要性？
lv2: 霍爾木茲海峽關閉為什麼會令全球油價上升？這對普通市民的生活有何影響？
巴基斯坦在美伊之間擔當調解角色，你認為一個第三方國家在國際衝突中可以如何發揮作用？
lv3: 有人認為國際制裁和軍事封鎖是解決國際衝突的有效手段，但亦有人認為這只會令平民百姓受苦。你如何評價這兩種觀點？
全球對石油的依賴令霍爾木茲海峽的局勢影響深遠。你認為各國應如何加快發展可再生能源，以減少類似危機的風險？
